agent_scope.summary: Section 13 registry consistency contracts plus discovered package-facing omissions not cleanly owned by sections 1-13.

contracts.title: Block registry descriptors preserve package identity
contracts.contract: A package validator must derive block-package consistency from BlockRegistry descriptors, not live class references: entry-point blocks must produce BlockSpec rows with stable module/class identity, package_name, type_name aliases, and package metadata exposed through packages() and specs_by_package().

code_evidence.notes: BlockSpec is the descriptor row used by registry consumers.
BlockRegistry owns package metadata and grouped spec accessors.
Public registry method delegates to the entry-point scan implementation.
Registry exposes package grouping derived from BlockSpec.package_name.

adr_evidence.pattern: BlockRegistry` entry-point scan accepts package tuples, plain block lists,
adr_evidence.notes: Draft records current BlockRegistry package scan behavior.

adr_evidence.pattern: package metadata vs entry-point owner names need one combined pass.
adr_evidence.notes: Draft identifies package metadata cross-surface consistency as incomplete.

contracts.notes: The registry implements the raw descriptor surface, but there is no unified package validation report that checks package metadata consistency before registration.
contracts.validator_implication: Development should report inconsistent package metadata and missing package grouping as errors; production install should block packages whose block descriptors cannot be reconciled with package metadata.

contracts.title: Block entry-point scans are tolerant runtime discovery, not install validation
contracts.contract: The package validator must run block entry-point discovery in an isolated dry-run registry and convert unsupported return shapes, non-Block items, abstract blocks, duplicate capabilities, and callable failures into structured validation findings instead of relying on live startup warning-and-skip behavior.

code_evidence.notes: Mechanical inventory records the block registry scan module as the scan surface.
Tier-2 scan selects the scistudio.blocks entry-point group.
Invalid runtime entry points are logged and skipped.
Development fallback mirrors package entry-point callable protocol.

adr_evidence.pattern: Registry scan is tolerant by design.
adr_evidence.notes: Draft distinguishes runtime tolerance from production install validation.

adr_evidence.pattern: isolated dry-run registry and atomic commit/quarantine semantics.
adr_evidence.notes: Draft calls out dry-run registry requirement.

tests.notes: Runtime behavior currently logs and continues.
Abstract blocks are skipped with a precise warning.

contracts.notes: Current code is deliberately resilient at app startup; the package validator must be stricter and side-effect-free.
contracts.validator_implication: Validator implementations should instantiate temporary registries and classify each skipped entry point as a package validation finding, with production blocking invalid required package surfaces.

contracts.title: Type registry discovery must reconcile package types with blocks and previewers
contracts.contract: A package validator must scan scistudio.types entry points into a dry-run TypeRegistry, validate returned DataObject subclasses and Meta constraints, and then use that registry to verify block port types, IO capability data_type values, and previewer target_type references.

code_evidence.notes: Mechanical inventory records the type registry module as the scan surface.
Type entry-point discovery is the package type scan hook.
Registry scans the scistudio.types entry-point group.

code_evidence.pattern: Entry-point '%s' type %r has a Meta class that violates
code_evidence.notes: Bad type Meta declarations are warning/skipped at runtime.

adr_evidence.notes: Draft records the package type entry-point protocol.

adr_evidence.pattern: block port types vs package type entry points, previewer target types vs
adr_evidence.notes: Draft identifies type registry cross-surface gaps.

tests.pattern: A DataObject subclass in a scanned dir is registered by scan_all.
tests.notes: Tests registry scan behavior for drop-in types.

contracts.notes: The type registry validates individual type classes, but current package validation does not yet perform a combined block/type/previewer consistency pass.
contracts.validator_implication: The validator should load candidate package types first, then reject package blocks, capabilities, or previewers that reference unresolved or inconsistent type names.

contracts.title: Previewer package discovery must feed cross-surface target validation
contracts.contract: A package validator must scan scistudio.previewers factories, reject malformed PreviewerSpec lists, duplicate or empty previewer_id values, unresolved target_type references, and owner/package mismatches, then reconcile previewer specs with the type registry and package metadata.

code_evidence.notes: Mechanical inventory records the previewer registry module as the scan surface.

code_evidence.pattern: def load_packages(self, *, include_monorepo: bool = False) -> None:
code_evidence.notes: Package previewers load through this registry method.
Entry-point scan covers the scistudio.previewers group.
Malformed factories are diagnostics, not hard install failures today.

adr_evidence.notes: Draft records the previewer entry-point protocol.

adr_evidence.pattern: Previewer spec validation is the weakest surface:
adr_evidence.notes: Draft calls out missing previewer package validation.

contracts.notes: PreviewerRegistry has coarse validation, but registry diagnostics currently do not resolve target_type or owner consistency against package metadata and TypeRegistry.
contracts.validator_implication: Production package validation should block malformed previewer specs and unresolved package previewer targets instead of allowing them to degrade to runtime diagnostics.

contracts.title: Code runner entry-point and language registry contract is not owned by sections 1-13
contracts.contract: The scistudio.runners entry-point group and RunnerRegistry language mapping are package-facing extension surfaces. A package validator needs an explicit contract for runner class importability, language-key normalization, required runner protocol methods, duplicate language handling, and production compatibility.

code_evidence.notes: The root package declares a runner entry-point group.
RunnerRegistry is the public language-to-runner registry.
Registry normalizes language keys but does not validate a package runner protocol.

tests.notes: Tests runner registry behavior but not external package validation.

contracts.notes: The survey draft focuses on blocks, types, previewers, IO, and preview surfaces. Runner entry points exist in package metadata but do not yet have a validator section.
contracts.validator_implication: Add a validator section or explicit section-02 subprofile for scistudio.runners before external runner packages are accepted in production.

contracts.title: Scaffold-generated package fixtures are a package contract
contracts.contract: The init-block-package scaffold generates pyproject metadata, scistudio.blocks entry-point wiring, a get_blocks() callable returning PackageInfo plus block classes, and package-local tests. The package validator should either consume scaffold smoke fixtures or declare scaffold validation out of scope.

code_evidence.notes: Scaffold creates package metadata and source/test files.
Template declares the block package entry point.
Template emits the preferred package callable protocol.
Template emits package-local entry-point tests.

adr_evidence.notes: Draft notes scaffold guidance and tests as existing coverage.

contracts.notes: Sections 1-13 cover the runtime surfaces that scaffold output targets, but not the scaffold-as-contract artifact itself.
contracts.validator_implication: Development validation should smoke-test scaffolded packages or reuse scaffold fixtures as conformance examples; production package validation can skip scaffold-only checks.

contracts.title: Generated public symbol facts are an API/package compatibility contract
contracts.contract: The generated facts pipeline records public Python symbols with griffe. Because package validators may claim API compatibility or public extension surfaces, ADR-049 should decide whether validator output must reference or update generated symbol facts for package-facing API drift.

code_evidence.notes: Public symbol facts are extracted from Python package surfaces.
Private symbols are excluded from generated public facts.

code_evidence.pattern: parser.add_argument("--check", action="store_true", help="Fail if docs/facts/generated.yaml is stale")
code_evidence.notes: Generated facts have an explicit stale-check command.

contracts.notes: Generated facts are governance evidence rather than a plugin runtime, but they are package-facing when validator claims mention public APIs.
contracts.validator_implication: Keep generated facts out of production install blocking unless ADR-049 explicitly adopts them; in development, surface drift as informational validator context.

contracts.title: Registry-derived API serialization is a package-facing contract
contracts.contract: Block and previewer registries feed HTTP and MCP schema payloads. Package validation should verify candidate registry descriptors are JSON-serializable and remain consistent across palette, schema, connection-validation, preview capability, and MCP schema surfaces before committing them to live registries.

code_evidence.notes: Block registry descriptors are serialized into the palette API.
Block registry descriptors are serialized into per-block schema APIs.
Previewer specs have a dedicated API wire model.

contracts.notes: Sections 1-13 describe registry and previewer contracts but do not make API wire serialization an explicit package validation row.
contracts.validator_implication: Production package validation should reject registry descriptors that cannot be converted into stable API/MCP payloads, especially non-serializable config schema, format capability, and previewer manifest fields.

omissions.title: scistudio.runners lacks a package-validator section
omissions.recommended_section: Add section 14 or extend section 02 with a code-runner entry-point subprofile.
omissions.notes: The entry-point group exists, but sections 1-13 do not define an external runner package contract.

omissions.title: Scaffold templates define a conformance fixture outside the runtime sections
omissions.recommended_section: Add a development-only scaffold conformance subsection under section 99 or section 02.
omissions.notes: Generated package tests are package-facing but not an install-time runtime surface.

omissions.title: Generated public symbol facts are not linked to package validation claims

evidence.pattern: parser.add_argument("--check", action="store_true", help="Fail if docs/facts/generated.yaml is stale")

omissions.recommended_section: Keep as governance-only context or add a development validation evidence subsection.
omissions.notes: Facts are not a runtime package validator dependency today, but they constrain public API drift claims.

omissions.title: Registry-derived API serialization is not explicit in sections 1-13
omissions.recommended_section: Add a cross-surface API serialization subsection under section 13 or section 99.
omissions.notes: Package descriptors must remain safe for API and MCP payloads, not only valid inside registries.
